description: You are a plant pathologist at an agricultural extension laboratory. Classify an unknown wheat pathogen by comparing its ITS sequence (~/UGENE_Data/agriculture/unknown_pathogen_ITS.fasta) against reference sequences (~/UGENE_Data/agriculture/reference_wheat_pathogens_ITS.fasta) in UGENE. Use a ClustalW multiple sequence alignment (gap open penalty: 10, gap extension penalty: 0.2) and a Maximum Likelihood phylogenetic tree (PhyML, HKY85 model, 100 bootstrap replicates) to identify the pathogen. Export the alignment to ~/UGENE_Data/agriculture/results/pathogen_alignment.phy (PHYLIP) and ~/UGENE_Data/agriculture/results/path

metadata.data_source: Real ITS sequences from NCBI GenBank: PZ014411.1 (F. graminearum), PV098983.1 (P. triticina), PV416552.1 (B. graminis), MH862992.1 (Z. tritici), OQ257148.1 (P. tritici-repentis), MH855829.1 (T. caries), PV827756.1 (U. tritici), PV849335.1 (R. cerealis), MH855056.1 (G. tritici), PZ027992.1 (B. sorokiniana). Unknown sample is a modified F. graminearum ITS with 3 SNPs.
